---
title: "CIPP Model Overview"
description: "Study note on CIPP Model Overview in Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications."
book_id: "evaluation-theory-models-applications"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Major section"
source_pages: "345"
citation_key: "stufflebeam_coryn_2014_evaluation_theory_models_applications"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:overview-of-the-chapter-2:c0002"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2014</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Source pages 345</span><span>Major section</span></div>
This note summarises the MCP research-library section "CIPP Model Overview" from Daniel L. Stufflebeam and Chris L. S. Coryn's *Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications* [@stufflebeam_coryn_2014_evaluation_theory_models_applications]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked retrieval records, not a substitute for the source text.

## Core Argument

The CIPP overview introduces context, input, process, and product evaluation as linked ways to support improvement and accountability.

## Study Summary

The CIPP overview introduces context, input, process, and product evaluation as linked ways to support improvement and accountability.

The model can be used formatively and summatively across sectors.

Its systems orientation asks evaluators to attend to stakeholders, values, procedures, and use.

## Key Concepts

- Context evaluation
- Input evaluation
- Process evaluation
- Product evaluation

## Source Location

- Section path: Part Three > Chapter 13: Daniel Stufflebeam's CIPP Model > Overview of the Chapter
- Evidence records: `chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:overview-of-the-chapter-2:c0002`

## Practical Implications

- Use context evaluation to clarify needs and goals.
- Use input evaluation to judge strategies and plans.
- Use process and product evaluation to track implementation and results.

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Concepts: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Map: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What is the main evaluation problem that CIPP Model Overview helps diagnose?
2. Which assumptions would need evidence before using this guidance in a live evaluation?
3. How would this change the way an evaluator scopes questions, methods, or reporting?
4. Where could the guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## References
